title: 10 best herbal teas for bad breath

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Mentha x piperita's essential oils contain compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath, such as Streptococcus and Lactobacillus.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tea's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, preventing the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
Digestive stimulant properties: Mentha x piperita's menthol content can help stimulate digestion, reducing symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and gas that can contribute to bad breath.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint tea helps to kill bacteria in the mouth, reducing the causes of bad breath. Additionally, the cooling sensation of peppermint tea helps to stimulate saliva production, which in turn washes away bacteria and food particles that can cause bad breath, leaving the mouth feeling fresh and clean. This makes peppermint tea a popular remedy for bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is a primary cause of bad breath.
Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the buildup of bacteria that cause bad breath.

Catechin: Catechins are phenolic compounds that have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth and prevent the formation of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain essential oils that help to kill bacteria in the mouth, reducing bad breath.
Stems: They contain menthol, a natural decongestant and antibacterial agent that helps to freshen breath.
Buds: They are rich in menthone, a compound that has antibacterial properties and helps to reduce mouth odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of mentha x piperita from your garden or a nearby field in the early morning.
Rinse the harvested leaves gently with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Steep one teaspoon of fresh or dried mentha x piperita leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup or teapot.
Serve the mentha x piperita tea hot or iced and enjoy the refreshing flavor and aroma.

remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce bad breath by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs), which are a primary cause of bad breath.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia teas have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the bacterial load in the mouth and gums, thereby reducing bad breath.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are primarily used due to their high concentration of medicinal compounds that help in reducing bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves are used as they contain volatile oils, which contribute to their antimicrobial properties and help in combating bad breath-causing bacteria.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried echinacea angustifolia root and flowers from a trusted herbalist or supplier.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried root and flowers for every 8 ounces of water.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy within 24 hours of preparation.

remedy_recipe: Peel 1-2 inches of the rhizome of zingiber officinale using a sharp knife.

Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the desired flavor is reached.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving hot or iced.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for bad breath are a natural remedy that helps combat halitosis by reducing bacteria and inflammation in the mouth. These teas typically contain antibacterial and antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of odor-causing microorganisms. The best herbal teas for bad breath include Mentha x piperita, Zingiber officinale, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinnamomum verum, Cymbopogon citratus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Thymus vulgaris, Salvia officinalis, and Thymus serpyllum. If left untreated, bad breath can lead to social isolation, decreased confidence, and strained relationships, ultimately affecting one's mental and emotional well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with bad breath is a blend of peppermint, sage, and eucalyptus. Peppermint tea helps to reduce bacteria in the mouth and freshen breath. Sage tea is known for its antibacterial properties, which combat the causes of bad breath. Eucalyptus tea has natural decongestant properties that help to clear sinuses and reduce inflammation in the throat. Drinking a warm tea made from these herbs can provide long-lasting relief from bad breath and leave your mouth feeling clean and fresh.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal teas are gum inflammation, mouth ulcers, and dry mouth. Peppermint tea is often used to alleviate gum inflammation and mouth ulcers, while sage tea is used to combat dry mouth and bad breath. Echinacea tea is also used to boost the immune system and fight off infections that can lead to bad breath. These teas are natural remedies to promote oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum teas helps with bad breath because its active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have antibacterial properties that effectively kill the bacteria responsible for causing bad breath. These bacteria, particularly in the mouth and on the tongue, feed on food particles and release volatile sulfur compounds as a byproduct, resulting in bad breath. Heat 1 cup of water to 200 degrees fahrenheit in a tea kettle for 5 minutes.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve for 1 minute.

The citronella oil present in the tea has deodorizing and antiseptic effects, effectively reducing the volatile sulfur compounds that contribute to bad breath. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the gums and mouth, reducing irritation and inflammation that can lead to bad breath. This combination of properties makes Cymbopogon citratus tea a natural and effective remedy for bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Citral: This terpene constituent helps with bad breath by reducing the growth of oral bacteria that cause halitosis.
Cymbopogonol: This phenolic constituent has antimicrobial properties, which help inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
Geraniol: This terpene constituent has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help reduce the presence of pathogens that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cymbopogon citratus leaves are commonly used to make teas for bad breath due to their high content of citral and geraniol, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat oral bacteria.
Stems: Cymbopogon citratus stems are used to make teas for bad breath due to their ability to reduce inflammation and fight oral bacteria, thus providing relief from bad breath.

Rhyzomes: Cymbopogon citratus rhyzomes are used to make teas for bad breath due to their high content of oils that have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Chop the cymbopogon citratus leaves and stems into small pieces to enhance infusion.
Combine the chopped cymbopogon citratus with 1 liter of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solid cymbopogon citratus particles.
Store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with bad breath because of its natural antibacterial properties. The tea contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, including those that cause halitosis. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tea help to reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. Regular consumption of this tea may help to freshen breath and improve overall oral health. It also helps to reduce bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps reduce bad breath by its anti-inflammatory properties, which can alleviate mouth inflammation and soothe gum irritation.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These phenolic compounds exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to bad breath.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid glycoside has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the production of volatile sulfur compounds in the mouth that cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and fight bacteria that cause bad breath.
Leaves: They possess antimicrobial properties that help to eliminate bacteria and freshen breath.
Flowers: They have been used in traditional medicine to treat oral health issues and have antiseptic properties that help to combat bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1-2 teaspoons of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the roots in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a clean cup.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of honey to sweeten the tea to taste.
Drink the tea immediately and discard any remaining liquid after 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with bad breath because it contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can be a major contributor to bad breath. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the mucous membranes, allowing for a decrease in bacteria growth and subsequent odor. Additionally, Echinacea's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria that can cause bad breath, making it a natural and effective solution for freshening breath. Regular consumption can lead to a healthier oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for causing bad breath.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the mouth and alleviate symptoms of bad breath.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth, contributing to fresher breath.

Leaves: They possess volatile oils that have antibacterial properties, which can help combat oral bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from the plant in the morning.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer at 105 f for 2 hours.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried echinacea purpurea with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to a comfortable temperature.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to enjoy the potential immune-boosting benefits of echinacea purpurea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas helps with bad breath because of its potent antibacterial properties, which target and eliminate the bacteria responsible for causing halitosis. The tea's active compound, thymol, has been shown to effectively reduce the growth of Streptococcus and other bacteria that contribute to bad breath. Additionally, thymus vulgaris tea's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe the gums and reduce inflammation in the mouth, creating an environment that is less conducive to the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath. This combination makes thymus vulgaris tea an effective natural remedy for bad breath.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Helps with bad breath by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria that cause halitosis.
Carvacrol: Contributes to bad breath relief by inhibiting the production of volatile sulfur compounds, which are major contributors to bad breath.
Rosmarinic acid: Plays a role in alleviating bad breath by its antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which help to reduce the impact of free radicals and bacterial growth in the mouth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used in teas due to their potent antibacterial properties, which help combat the bacteria responsible for bad breath.
Stems: The stems of Thyme contain essential oils that freshen breath and reduce bacterial growth, making them a popular choice for teas.
Flowers: Thyme flowers have a strong, pleasant fragrance and contain oils that help kill bacteria in the mouth, making them a popular ingredient in teas for bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the early morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves per cup of boiling water for brewing.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to serve the thymus vulgaris tea hot or iced.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas helps with bad breath because of its antibacterial properties, which combat the underlying causes of halitosis. The tea's active compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, specifically those responsible for volatile sulfur compounds, the primary culprits behind bad breath. Additionally, Salvia officinalis contains antimicrobial properties that help reduce plaque and gum inflammation, further contributing to its effectiveness in freshening breath and promoting oral health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce the production of volatile sulfur compounds, which are the primary cause of bad breath.
Thujone: A terpene that has antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate bacteria in the mouth that can contribute to bad breath.
Carvacrol: A phenolic compound that has antibacterial properties, reducing the growth of bacteria that can cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for teas to alleviate bad breath due to their antibacterial properties that help reduce gum inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth.
Stems: Used for teas to alleviate bad breath due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties that help combat oral infections and freshen breath.
Roots: Used for teas to alleviate bad breath due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of salvia officinalis in the morning when the plant is at its peak oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on a paper bag for 1 to 2 weeks.
Grind 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Steep the ground leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes to release the oils.
Strain the tea using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve warm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas helps with bad breath because it contains thymol, a natural antibacterial compound that inhibits the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. Thyme's potent essential oils, including carvacrol and borneol, also contribute to its breath-freshening properties. The tea's ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria helps to alleviate symptoms of bad breath, such as gum inflammation and mouth odor. Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps combat bad breath by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth, ultimately minimizing bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their strong antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: They are used because of their fragrance and ability to freshen breath, making them a popular ingredient in mouthwashes and teas.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to act as a mild antibacterial agent, helping to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried thymus serpyllum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water for preparation.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste to sweeten the tea and enhance the flavor profile naturally.
Allow the tea to cool and then refrigerate it for up to 24 hours for later consumption.
